api.reportTemplateRemoved: The report template was successfully removed
api.reportTemplateAdded: The report template was added successfully
api.reportTemplateEdited: The report template was edited successfully
api.emailQueuedForDelivery: Report was generated and the email is queued for delivery
api.htmlTemplateRemoved: The HTML template was successfully removed
api.dataQueryRemoved: The data query was successfully removed
api.reportScheduleRan: Report schedule was run. Check the report history for the status.
api.reportHistoryDeleted: Report history entry was deleted successfully

runReportDialog.noTemplatesMessage: There are no report templates that depend on {typeDisplay}. You must select a dependency in the report template of type {type} using the dependencies dropdown.

templateImport.title: Import Report Template
templateImport.importFileHint: Only accepts exported report template JSON files

variablesSelector.blockNotFound: Block not found in template. Click on the block to copy and paste into template
variablesSelector.missingDependency: Missing value for dependency: {dep}. Open Preview to set values

editorToolbar.heading: Heading {level}
editorToolbar.noDataSources: No data sources exist in template variables

assets.title: Report Assets
assets.deleteMessage: You are about to delete {count} {item}. This action isn't reversible

assetSelect.title: Report Asset Select
assetSelect.noAssetsFound: No report assets found. Go to Reporting Manager and use the Report Assets button to upload

dataQuerySelect.title: Data Query Select
dataQuerySelect.selectQueryFromDropdown: Select a query from the dropdown

dependencyPrompt.title: Select Report Dependencies
dependencyPrompt.allFieldsRequired: All fields must have a value

dataQueryForm.name: Data Query Name
dataQueryForm.jsonParseError: There was an error parsing the JSON: {error}

dataQueryTable.title: Data Queries
dataQueryTable.deleteMessage: If this query is in use you will need to change it in every report template

htmlTemplateTable.title: Base Templates
htmlTemplateTable.deleteMessage: If this template is in use you will need to change it in every report template

historyTable.title: Report History
historyTable.deleteMessage: This will remove the history entry permanently.

scheduleForm.timezoneHint: Default timezone {timezone} will be used if blank
scheduleForm.noRecipientsAdded: No recipients added yet. Emails will be sent to the default recipients from global settings. Adding recipients here will override those defaults.
scheduleForm.dependenciesRequired: All required dependencies must be set before {action}.

emailSettingsForm.subject: Subject
emailSettingsForm.subjectHint: Default if left blank: 'Scheduled Report: <report name>'
emailSettingsForm.bodyHint: Default if blank: 'Your report is attached.'
emailSettingsForm.attachmentExtensionHint: Enter the desired extension without a dot (e.g., csv, json, txt). Defaults to 'txt' if blank.
emailSettingsForm.includeReportLink: Include a direct link to view the report online (requires TRMM account authentication).
emailSettingsForm.includeReportLinkTooltip: The link will be automatically added at the end of the email body.

providerCallback.loginError: There was an error logging into your provider.

settings.title: SSO Settings
settings.blockLocalUserLoginHint: When enabled, only users with SSO accounts can log in, with the exception of local superuser accounts.

providersTable.title: SSO Providers
providersTable.enableSsoToAddProvider: Enable SSO in the settings to allow adding a provider.

providersForm.providerNameValidation: Only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores are allowed
providersForm.providerNameHint: A unique identifier for the SSO provider. Avoid spaces and special characters, as this will be part of the callback URL.
providersForm.issuerUrlHint: The OpenID Connect Issuer URL provided by the SSO provider. This is typically the base URL where the provider hosts their OIDC configuration.
providersForm.defaultUserRoleHint: The role assigned to users upon first sign-in through this provider.

helpMenu.dataQueryDescription: Data Queries are used to save common database queries to use them in templates. Behind the scenes, we are just creating a Django queryset. The only difference is these querysets are restricted to only retrieve data versus modifying data.
helpMenu.syntaxDescription: When you create Data Queries in the Data Query Editor you use JSON. You can also create Data Queries directly in the template variables which uses YAML syntax.
helpMenu.structureDescription: Ctrl+Space in the query editor to auto-complete values
helpMenu.modelFieldDescription: This is the only required field. This specifies the table to query.
